schema_note: 5-tier source classification + freshness periods. Used by source-validator.mjs to grade every datum, and by synthesizer to gate major recommendations. Major recommendations (school choice, PR feasibility) must NOT rely solely on tier ≥ 4 sources.

tier_1.name: Government / university primary
tier_1.description: First-party official source. Government agency or university publishing the data itself.
examples: University course pages (e.g. CDU, UTAS, La Trobe)
Home Affairs SkillSelect invitation rounds page
tier_1.trust: highest — use as primary evidence for any major recommendation

tier_2.name: Statutory / regulatory body
tier_2.description: Authoritative body publishing in their statutory role (not the regulator, but a body established by statute to publish specific data).
examples: Occupational Therapy Board of Australia (OTBA) communiques
Occupational Therapy Council (OTC) — designated assessing authority
Department of Education — higher education completion rates
Jobs and Skills Australia — Skills Priority List
tier_2.trust: high — use as primary evidence; minor delay between event and publication is normal

tier_3.name: Industry / professional body
tier_3.description: Industry association, professional society, or not-for-profit research org.
tier_3.trust: medium-high — good for context, but check the underlying primary source before using for major decisions

tier_4.name: Third-party aggregator / analysis
tier_4.description: Commercial or volunteer site that republishes / analyzes primary data. The site adds interpretation or aggregation, not the underlying source.
examples: Visa Sidekick (visasidekick.com.au — republishes DHA official lists)
SmartVisaGuide (smartvisaguide.com — paid tier with DHA data)
KBA Global / EasyMigrate / CanApprove / Mediix (migration agency blogs)
tier_4.trust: medium — useful for orientation, but NOT primary evidence. Major recommendations must NOT rely solely on tier 4.
tier_4.common_issue: Tertiary interpretation of DHA data may add 1-3 day lag, paraphrase inaccurate, or apply generic formulas to specific occupations

tier_5.name: Social media / forums
tier_5.description: User-generated content. NOT to be used as evidence in any conclusion.
examples: Poms in Oz / Canadians in Aus
tier_5.trust: lowest — explicitly BANNED from major recommendations. May be used as orientation signal only with explicit ⚠️ caveat.

freshness_explanation: Days after which a datum should be re-verified. The validator auto-degrades ✅ to ⚠️ when age exceeds the freshness period for its data type. SkillSelect cutoffs are most volatile (45 days); scholarship % stable for 1 year.

output_discipline.rule_1_each_checkmark_needs_date: Every ✅ label must be immediately followed by the retrieval date. ✅ without date = 'unverified'.
output_discipline.rule_2_no_tier_5_in_conclusions: tier 5 (social media) may not appear as evidence in any recommendation or conclusion.
output_discipline.rule_3_no_tier_4_alone_for_major: Major recommendations (school choice, PR feasibility) must NOT rely on tier 4 alone. Either cross-reference tier 1, or label as 'pending official confirmation'.
output_discipline.rule_4_explicit_tier_and_status: Every datum used in output should carry both tier and status (✅/⚠️/❌). The validator assigns these; the synthesizer respects them.
output_discipline.rule_5_validator_cannot_be_disabled: Source-validator.mjs is the gate before synthesis. Cannot be skipped except in an explicit --no-validate override (logged).

verification_examples.good: CDU Master of OT — tuition $34,736/y ✅ tier_1 (CDU official) — retrieved 2026-06-09
SkillSelect 13 Nov 2025 OT cutoff 75 — tier_4 (Visa Sidekick republishing DHA) ⚠️ awaiting tier_1 cross-verification — retrieved 2026-06-09
verification_examples.bad: CDU Master of OT — tuition $34,736/y ✅  (no date, no tier)
OT cutoff 65  (no source, no tier, no date — forbidden)
User said on Reddit 189 will be 90 next year ❌ (tier 5, banned)

domain_tiers._comment: v0.3.1: explicit domain → tier mapping. Used by source-validator.mjs instead of hardcoded const. Future tier additions: edit this file, no code change needed. Tier rules: .gov.au/.edu.au primary; .com.au/.org.au = evaluate per source; reddit/facebook/x = tier_5.
domain_tiers._ordering: longest domain first when ambiguous (e.g. immi.homeaffairs.gov.au before homeaffairs.gov.au).

tier_1.immi.homeaffairs.gov.au: Department of Home Affairs — SkillSelect, EOI calculator, visa subclasses
tier_1.homeaffairs.gov.au: DHA (parent domain, fallback for other DHA pages)
tier_1.otcouncil.com.au: Occupational Therapy Council (assessing authority, not regulator)
tier_1.cricos.education.gov.au: CRICOS — Commonwealth Register of Institutions and Courses
tier_1.qilt.edu.au: Quality Indicators for Learning and Teaching
tier_1.jobsandskills.gov.au: Jobs and Skills Australia — Skills Priority List
tier_1.hwd.health.gov.au: Department of Health — Health Workforce Data

tier_2.studymelbourne.vic.gov.au: VIC Government study portal (paraphrase of uni data, not source-of-truth)

tier_3._comment: Currently no tier_3 sources in v0.3.1 data. Add here when OTA / industry bodies are cited.

tier_4.visasidekick.com.au: Visa Sidekick — republishes DHA official lists
tier_4.smartvisaguide.com: SmartVisaGuide — paid tier with DHA data

tier_5.youtube.com: YouTube (treat as user content unless official channel)
